channel.title: Waldi Lane Farm
channel.description: Recent content on Waldi Lane Farm

item.title: Cookie
item.description: Cookie is a sweet girl with a lovely structure. She is a 4th generation and naturally polled.

item.title: Trixi
item.description: Trixi is still a kid but so far, she is a near carbon copy of her dam Moderna. She will be bred this year and we are excited to see the udder that comes from the Moderna / Oh cross. We anticipate Trixi and/or Angel will easily contribute to Oh’s +B. She is a 3rd generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Angel
item.description: Angel is a lovely girl. She is bred to an excellent nigerian dwarf for her first kidding in 2026. We are hopeful to see a beautiful correct udder on this girl. So far, Oh has added capacity and wide orifices. Angel is a 3rd generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Vivi
item.description: Vivi throws absolutely lovely daughters. Viv is a bit steep in the rump, but paired with level bucks, her girls have had really nice conformation. She is a heavy producer at a gallon a day. She is a 3rd generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Moderna
item.description: Moderna is flashy and a milking powerhouse at over a gallon a day. She is an excellent mother and will take on any bottle fails that I accidentally have. She loves babies. Moderna has thrown doelings every year and they are always covered in spots. This girl is my easiest keeper too. She is a 3rd generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Pupper
item.description: Pupper is just as her name indicates. She is calm and cuddly and will follow us anywhere. I tried for 2 years to get a doe out of the Clara / Champ cross. I had quads to choose from. All of them were nice, but I was drawn to Pupper for her structure and temperament. Pups is a Grade Mini Nubian.

item.title: Sidda
item.description: Sidda is the barn favorite. She has the sweetest temperament and will follow us around like a faithful dog. She had a few podium placings in 2025 shows as a first freshener. Sidda’s second freshening should be exciting. Her udder has a lovely shape and nice snug attachments. She is a 4th generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Whitney
item.description: Whitney has an independent personality. All pictures are candid only. We love her as a daughter out of our sweet Sidda and we are thrilled to see what a Sidda / Oh udder looks like when she kids in 2026. She has gorgeous breed character with ears for days and a perfect roman nose. She is a 5th generation Mini Nubian

item.title: Ash
item.description: Ash has Bunny’s eyes and structure. Bunny is a high producer and Champ comes from a line of high producers. I do hope Champ improves attachments and udder height. I wish kids were easier to photograph. Ash is quite level on the move and really any time when she isn’t being as stubborn as a goat. Ash is a 3rd generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Gigi
item.description: Gigi is flighty. She has yet to give me a doeling too. I just love how snug her udder is. It is soft as butter and she has nice wide orifices. Think pink for 2026! Gigi is a 3rd generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Jilly
item.description: Jilly is a lovely nubian doe. She is wide and level and just sweet as pie. She has amazing lateral attachments and produced well for her first freshening. She is bred to a nigerian for new foundation mini nubians.

item.title: Tika
item.description: Tika is a gorgeous Nigerian Dwarf. She is level and wide. She has a perfect brisket and proper rear leg angulation. Her best characteristic is her personality. She knows she is adorable. She is another one of my 2025 kids that loves to follow me around. She comes from a wonderful line of high performers. Her dam, Savy is my favorite showing partner and I look forward to showing Tika in 2026. Thank you to Kate at Floof Farm for selling this gorgeous girl to me.

item.title: Latte
item.description: Latte is an exciting prospect out of my grade project. Her dam’s production and orifices were an improvement. We expect Champ to improve attachments and add on even more capacity. Once again, Latte is lovely on the move and when she isn’t protesting being told to stand nice and relax. Latte is a 1st generation Mini Nubian per the rules of emerging out of the Grade herdbook.

item.title: Lulu
item.description: Lulu is an exciting prospect. She is out of my highest producer, Soaring Heart’s Moderna and is the granddaughter of Green Gables Southern Belle 4*P. She will have her first freshening this year and we are very excited to see her udder. She is a 3rd generation Mini Nubian

item.title: Blossom
item.description: Blossom is a doe out of my grade project. Her granddam was my very first mini nubian and her sire is my favorite buck, Oh. She freshened in 2025 too late in the season for me to milk her through but she came out strong at over 5lbs per day. I love the soft texture of her udder and Oh added amazing orifices. She is a grade Mini Nubian.

item.title: Celeste
item.description: Celeste is a kind doe and has a lovely feminine structure plus her granddam’s width. Her first freshening udder was a very nice shape. I am hoping for increased capacity in her second freshening. If she can earn her star in 2026, she will be an 8 star milker. She is a 3rd generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Bunny
item.description: Bunny is super chill and loves head scritches. She will throw down almost a gallon a day at peak production. She is a 2nd generation Mini Nubian

item.title: Rue
item.description: Rue has good width and the shorter stature that I like in mini nubians. I love her front end structure and despite the picture, she really has a level rump. She was convinced nothing good comes of being outside the barn. She has excellent genetics on both sides and I am very excited to see her kids and her udder. She is a 6th generation Mini Nubian

item.title: Marvin
item.description: Marvin is another barn favorite. She’s a seet girl that loves a head scratch and is happy to follow the people anywhere. I am super excited for her to freshen in March 2026. I anticipate that she will easily contribute to her sire’s +B. She is a 4th generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: How to Make a Mini Nubian
item.description: Minis are a dairy breed. They are a cross between a standard size dairy doe such as Alpine, La Mancha, Toggenburg, Saanen, or Nubian with a Nigerian dwarf buck. Why do such a thing when we have these long standing, well recognized breeds? As it turns out, size matters. In these days of expensive feed and space challenges, large goats take up too much space and eat too much. They can produce a lot of milk too. Really small goats produce less and can be more challenging to milk. Enter the Goldilocks of goats – the mini breeds.

item.title: Champ
item.description: Champ is a handsome guy. He has a wonderful temperament. This will be the first year we get to see what kind of udders he puts on daughters. I have have high hopes because he has a phenomenal, productive dam. He is a 2nd generation Mini Nubian.

item.title: Oh
item.description: Oh is a wonderful guy. He has correct structure and good width. He is on the smaller side and his kids tend to be on the smaller side. He gives them amazing ears and often throws his blue eyes. He specializes in chocolate and buckskin patterns. She is a 6th generation Mini Nubian
